criteria: The agent has read Section 2.1 completely to understand the overall IGEGRNS framework, including the two main modules: Learning gene node embedding and Predicting gene interactions.
The agent has read Section 2.1.1 completely to understand GraphSAGE-based gene node embedding, including mean aggregation and the l-hop neighbor aggregation formulas.
The agent has read Section 2.1.1 to understand the Top-k pooling strategy, including equations (3)-(6) defining the projection, scoring, selection, and adjacency submatrix operations.

The agent has read Section 2.1.2 completely to understand the CNN-based gene interaction prediction module, including the 3-layer stacked CNN, BatchNorm, max pooling, fully connected layer, and sigmoid scoring.

The agent has read Section 2.2 completely to understand the six scRNA-seq datasets (hESC, hHEP, mESC, mHSC-E, mHSC-GM, mHSC-L), the 5-fold cross-validation setup, and the positive/negative edge sampling strategy.
The agent has read Section 2.3 completely to understand the AUROC and AUPRC evaluation metrics and their calculation formulas (Equations 9-12).

The agent has read Section 3.1 to understand all implementation hyperparameters: output dimension 256, MEAN aggregator, 1-hop neighbors, k=1 for Top-k pooling, CNN kernel size 2, initial learning rate 0.01, and learning rate decay schedule (80% every 10 epochs).
The agent has read Figure 3 to understand the benchmark comparison results of IGEGRNS against nine competing methods on six scRNA-seq datasets with both 500 and 1000 TFs.
The agent has read Tables 1 and 2 to understand the ablation study comparing MEAN/LSTM/MAX aggregators, 3-layer vs 1-layer CNN, and Top-k pooling vs without Top-k pooling.
The agent has read Figure 4 to understand the embedding vector dimensionality experiment comparing sizes 64, 128, 256, and 512 on the six scRNA-seq datasets.

The agent has read Section 3.4 to understand the real dataset validation on MEF-to-myofibroblast (405 cells) and hESC differentiation (758 cells) datasets, including the 20% positive sampling protocol and reported AUROC/AUPRC values.

The plan includes loading and preprocessing the six scRNA-seq datasets (hESC, hHEP, mESC, mHSC-E, mHSC-GM, mHSC-L) by selecting the top 500 and 1000 most differential genes following the BEELINE framework.
The plan includes constructing positive and negative edges from the reference network and implementing 5-fold cross-validation with 4 parts training and 1 part test.
The plan includes implementing the GraphSAGE node embedding module with MEAN aggregation over 1-hop neighbors as defined in Equations (1) and (2).
The plan includes implementing the Top-k pooling module using Equations (3)-(6), selecting the top k=1 most influential gene nodes.
The plan includes constructing the input matrix E_{i,j} for each gene pair as a (k+2) x d matrix combining gene i embedding, gene j embedding, and k Top-k pooling node embeddings as in Equation (7).
The plan includes implementing the 3-layer stacked CNN prediction module with BatchNorm, convolutional layers, max pooling, and a fully connected layer with sigmoid output.
The plan includes training with binary cross-entropy loss (Equation 8) with learning rate 0.01 decaying to 80% every 10 epochs.
The plan includes implementing AUROC and AUPRC evaluation metrics using 5-fold cross-validation results averaged across folds.
The plan includes comparing IGEGRNS against nine baselines: GENIE3, GRNBoost2, PIDC, SCODE, DeepSEM (unsupervised) and CNNC, GNE, DeepRIG, DGRNs (supervised).
The plan includes ablation experiments: comparing MEAN vs LSTM vs MAX aggregators, 3-layer stacked CNN vs 1-layer CNN, and with vs without Top-k pooling.
The plan includes the embedding dimensionality experiment using four embedding sizes (64, 128, 256, 512) to validate the choice of dimension 256.
The plan includes the real dataset validation experiment on MEF-to-myofibroblast and hESC differentiation datasets with 20% positive and equal negative sampling.
The code for loading the six scRNA-seq datasets (hESC, hHEP, mESC, mHSC-E, mHSC-GM, mHSC-L) and their corresponding reference networks has been implemented.
The code for selecting the top 500 and 1000 most differentially expressed genes from each scRNA-seq dataset has been implemented, following the BEELINE preprocessing protocol.
The code for generating positive edges (from the reference network) and negative edges (edges not in the reference network) and mixing them for 5-fold cross-validation has been implemented.
The code for the mean aggregation function (Equation 1), computing the d-dimensional average of l-hop neighbor node embeddings, has been implemented.

The code for the GraphSAGE node embedding update (Equation 2), applying ReLU activation on the concatenation of the current embedding and the aggregated neighbor embedding multiplied by weight matrix W, has been implemented.

The code for the Top-k pooling scoring step (Equation 3), computing the projection of the embedding matrix X onto the learnable vector p normalized by its L2 norm followed by ReLU activation, has been implemented.
The code for selecting the top-k node indices from the scoring vector (Equation 4) has been implemented.
The code for extracting the selected node feature matrix X' by element-wise multiplication of X with tanh of the scoring vector and indexing by idx (Equation 5) has been implemented.
The code for extracting the submatrix A' of the adjacency matrix indexed by the selected top-k node indices (Equation 6) has been implemented.
The code for constructing the input matrix E_{i,j} as a (k+2) x d matrix by concatenating the embedding of gene i, gene j, and the k top-k pooling node embeddings (Equation 7) has been implemented.

The code for the 3-layer stacked CNN prediction module has been implemented, where each feature extraction layer consists of a BatchNorm layer, a CNN convolutional layer with kernel size 2, and a max pooling layer.

The code for concatenating the outputs of the three CNN feature extraction layers and feeding them into the fully connected layer (lazy linear + ordinary linear) with sigmoid activation to produce predicted regulatory scores has been implemented.
The binary cross-entropy loss function (Equation 8) has been implemented for training the IGEGRNS model.
The code for computing AUROC and AUPRC evaluation metrics using the formulas for FPR, TPR, Precision, and Recall (Equations 9-12) has been implemented.
The GraphSAGE output embedding dimension has been set to 256, the aggregation function to MEAN, and neighbor aggregation to 1-hop in the implementation.
The Top-k pooling parameter k has been set to 1 and the CNN convolution kernel size has been set to 2 in the implementation.
The initial learning rate has been set to 0.01 with a decay schedule reducing the learning rate to 80% of its value every 10 training epochs.
The 5-fold cross-validation training loop has been implemented, averaging AUROC and AUPRC results across all five folds to produce the final scores.
The code for ablation experiments comparing MEAN, LSTM, and MAX aggregation functions in GraphSAGE has been implemented.
The code for ablation experiments comparing the default 3-layer stacked CNN with a simplified 1-layer CNN in the prediction module has been implemented.
The code for ablation experiments comparing Top-k pooling (k=1) versus removing Top-k pooling entirely from the model has been implemented.
The code for the embedding dimensionality experiment comparing vector sizes of 64, 128, 256, and 512 on the six datasets has been implemented.

The code for loading the MEF-to-myofibroblast dataset (405 cells, measured at 0/2/5/22 days) and the hESC differentiation dataset (758 cells, measured at 0/12/24/36/72/96 h), selecting top 100 genes with large expression differences for each, has been implemented.

The code for the real dataset validation protocol has been implemented, randomly selecting 20% of real directed regulatory edges as positive samples with equal-sized negative samples as training data.

The main benchmark experiment comparing IGEGRNS against all nine baseline methods (GENIE3, GRNBoost2, PIDC, SCODE, DeepSEM, CNNC, GNE, DeepRIG, DGRNs) on all six scRNA-seq datasets with 500 TFs has been executed successfully without errors.
The main benchmark experiment with 1000 TFs on all six scRNA-seq datasets has been executed successfully without errors.
All ablation study experiments (aggregator selection, CNN depth, Top-k pooling) on the six datasets with 1000 TFs have been executed successfully without errors.
The embedding dimensionality experiment (sizes 64, 128, 256, 512) on the six datasets with 500 TFs has been executed successfully without errors.
The real dataset validation experiments on the MEF-to-myofibroblast and hESC differentiation datasets have been executed successfully without errors.
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: IGEGRNS achieves the highest AUROC on all six scRNA-seq datasets for both 500 and 1000 TF configurations.
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: IGEGRNS achieves the highest AUPRC on five out of six datasets (all except mESC) for both 500 and 1000 TF configurations.
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: with 500 TFs, IGEGRNS achieves an average AUROC improvement of approximately 5.6% and AUPRC improvement of approximately 6.7% over the second-ranked algorithm.

The execution outcomes match Table 1: with the MEAN aggregator and 1000 TFs, IGEGRNS achieves AUROC values of approximately 0.863 (hHEP), 0.842 (mHSC-L), 0.841 (mESC), 0.915 (mHSC-E), 0.915 (mHSC-GM), and 0.808 (hESC).

The execution outcomes match Table 2: with the MEAN aggregator and 1000 TFs, IGEGRNS achieves AUPRC values of approximately 0.781 (hHEP), 0.840 (mHSC-L), 0.733 (mESC), 0.936 (mHSC-E), 0.937 (mHSC-GM), and 0.521 (hESC).
The execution outcomes match Tables 1 and 2: the LSTM and MAX aggregators both underperform the MEAN aggregator across all six datasets in both AUROC and AUPRC.
The execution outcomes match Tables 1 and 2: the 1-layer CNN underperforms the 3-layer stacked CNN across all six datasets in both AUROC and AUPRC.
The execution outcomes match Tables 1 and 2: removing Top-k pooling results in lower AUROC and AUPRC compared to Top-k pooling with k=1 across the six datasets.
The execution outcomes match Figure 4: IGEGRNS performance peaks at embedding dimension 256 and declines with both smaller (64, 128) and larger (512) dimensions on the six scRNA-seq datasets.
The execution outcomes match Section 3.4: the AUROC of IGEGRNS on the MEF-to-myofibroblast dataset is approximately 0.672 and on the hESC differentiation dataset is approximately 0.741.
The execution outcomes match Section 3.4: the AUPRC of IGEGRNS on the MEF-to-myofibroblast dataset is approximately 0.447 and on the hESC differentiation dataset is approximately 0.452.
